context.product: Collaborative project workspace for planning work, assigning owners, and completing shared workflows; expected weekly use
context.customer: Operations and project leads in self-serve teams with 5-20 intended seats; department manager pays; the team benefits; current alternative is spreadsheets plus chat
context.business_model: Self-serve subscription charged per active seat; gross margin is mainly affected by support and infrastructure usage
context.market: English-speaking SMB and lower-mid-market teams using horizontal work-management software

outcome.metric: Retained activation: at least two workspace members complete one shared workflow within 7 days, followed by a value-bearing workflow event in both week 2 and week 4
outcome.segment: New self-serve workspaces with 5-20 intended seats, excluding employees, tests, and sales-assisted accounts
outcome.time_window: Planned enrollment from 2026-07-27 through 2026-08-31; interim continue or stop-for-safety decision on 2026-08-20; final ship decision no earlier than 2026-10-02 after the August 31 cohort has 28 days of observation and data processing is complete. If fewer than 1,900 eligible workspaces enroll by August 31, continue enrollment and move the final decision to at least 32 days after the target is reached.
outcome.symptom: Signup volume holds near 3,200 per month and registration completion is 91%, but collaborative first value is 24% and retained activation is 14%.

metrics.name: Eligible 5-20-seat self-serve share
metrics.segment: New workspaces with known intended seats and account type

metrics.name: Retained activation, imported or integrated start
metrics.segment: Imported or integrated data in first session

metrics.name: Trial-to-paid, collaborative first value not reached

constraint.primary: New teams fail to reach collaborative first value: a second member joins and the team completes a real shared workflow within 7 days
constraint.mechanism: Blank-state setup, generic sample content, and invitation timing leave the initiating user doing solo configuration. Teammates lack relevant context when invited, shared work does not begin, and the workspace has no recurring coordination value to retain.
constraint.selection_rationale: Registration completes at 91%, while collaborative first value reaches only 24%. Reaching collaborative first value is associated with materially higher retained activation and paid conversion, varies sharply by setup intent, and can be tested within 30 days; the controlled experiment tests causality and selection effects.

alternative_constraints.reason_not_primary: Registration completion is 91%, leaving too little reachable loss to explain the 14% retained-activation baseline; device-level abandonment remains unaudited.
Imported or integrated starts activate much better and interviews more often cite setup and teammate context, but the contradictory solo-template retention signal keeps this alternative open.
Retained activation is weak across the three largest channels and the imported-versus-blank path difference persists within channel; channel incrementality and intent mix remain missing.
Known import latency and permission errors affect 8% of new workspaces and do not explain most of the collaborative-value gap.

supporting.finding: Registration completion is 91%, but only 24% of eligible signups reach collaborative first value and 14% reach retained activation; the largest value-bearing loss occurs after initial setup.
supporting.source: Signup, invitation, workflow, and cohort events
supporting.quality_notes: Event coverage is 96%; intended seat count is self-reported and missing for 11% of signups.

supporting.finding: Retained activation is 24% for workspaces that import or integrate real data in the first session versus 7% for blank-workspace starts.
supporting.segment: New workspaces with known setup path
supporting.quality_notes: Observational; stronger intent or prior preparation may select users into import or integration.

supporting.finding: Retained activation is 22% for 11-20 intended seats and 11% for 5-10 intended seats; both groups complete registration above 89%.
supporting.quality_notes: Larger teams may have a more urgent coordination need and are not directly comparable.

supporting.finding: Workspaces with 5-20 intended seats account for 64% of classifiable self-serve signups. At the observed 3,200 monthly signup volume, the planned July 27-August 31 window is expected to yield more than 1,900 eligible workspaces before exclusions.
supporting.segment: New self-serve workspaces with known intended seats and account type
supporting.quality_notes: Intended seats are self-reported, 11% of signups lack the field, and future mix may change; enrollment continues if the observed target is missed.

supporting.finding: Retained activation is 13% for direct, 15% for organic search, and 12% for partner-referred signups. Within each of these three largest attributed channels, imported or integrated starts outperform blank starts by 12-16 percentage points.
supporting.source: Attribution joined to workflow cohort model
supporting.quality_notes: Last-touch attribution is missing for 17% of signups, channel intent differs, and the partner segment has only 184 mature workspaces; this weakens but does not remove acquisition quality as an alternative.

supporting.finding: In 9 of 12 interviews with unactivated workspace creators, users delayed invitations because the workspace contained generic sample data, was not ready to show, or gave teammates no clear first task.
supporting.segment: Workspace creators who failed to reach collaborative first value
supporting.quality_notes: Small, purposive sample; recall and interviewer bias are possible.

supporting.finding: Trials reaching collaborative first value convert to paid at 36%, compared with 7% when the event is not reached; 90-day seat expansion is 18% versus 3%.
supporting.quality_notes: Association is not causal; high-intent teams may both collaborate and pay. Refund and support-cost differences are not included.

contradictory.finding: Among workspace creators who used a role-specific template but invited nobody in week 1, 28% returned for a value-bearing solo workflow in week 4, above the 19% week-4 return rate for all new workspace creators.
contradictory.quality_notes: This selected group is 9% of workspace creators and does not meet the collaborative retained-activation definition; solo value may be a valid precursor for some intents.

contradictory.finding: Known import-latency or permission failures appear in 8% of new-workspace sessions, and affected workspaces reach retained activation at 6%.
contradictory.source: Support logs and client-error events joined to workflow cohorts
contradictory.segment: New workspaces with a detected setup failure
contradictory.quality_notes: Support logs undercount silent failures, client-error coverage is incomplete, and affected users may attempt more complex setups; reliability could be primary for this subset.

missing_evidence: We lack randomized evidence that accelerating collaborative first value causes retained activation rather than identifying high-intent teams; this is needed before making the new path the default for all self-serve signups.
We lack reliable role and job-to-be-done classification for 43% of new workspaces; this is needed to decide whether the solo-template counter-segment should receive a different activation path.

We lack teammate-level qualitative evidence from invitees who ignored or accepted invitations; this is needed to distinguish weak invitation context from low underlying need before investing in notification changes.

We lack complete import latency, permission-error, and support-cost data joined to activation cohorts; this is needed to decide whether reliability work should displace the proposed onboarding intervention.

priorities.action: Instrument one activation scorecard by setup path, intended seats, role, acquisition channel, invite state, collaborative first value, and week-2/week-4 value events; audit import and permission failures.
priorities.rationale: Resolve the most decision-relevant missing evidence and prevent aggregate funnel gains from hiding weak retained value.
priorities.success_signal: At least 95% event coverage, cohort reconciliation within 2 percentage points, and a documented readout for every priority segment.
dependencies: Identity, workflow, billing, and support joins
guardrails: Do not collect message content or customer artifact contents
Do not publish a segment cut when its denominator is below 50 workspaces

priorities.action: Run a controlled activation-path experiment that asks for the real workflow, creates a role-relevant starting workspace, and invites one named teammate into a preassigned first task.
priorities.rationale: Test whether reducing solo setup and improving invitation context causes collaborative first value and retained activation.
priorities.success_signal: On 2026-08-20 the team makes only a continue or stop-for-safety decision. The final decision uses mature week-4 retained activation after at least 1,900 eligible workspaces enroll and the last included cohort matures; if the target is missed on August 31, enrollment and the decision date both extend.
dependencies: Approximately 1,710 analyzed after no more than 10% exclusion or attrition
guardrails: Stop enrollment for safety if setup completion falls by more than 5 percentage points
Stop enrollment for safety if invite complaints exceed 0.5%
Stop enrollment for safety if severe permission errors exceed 1% of exposed workspaces
Do not ship from the interim readout
Do not substitute proxy improvement for mature retained activation

priorities.action: Interview 8 initiating users and 8 invitees across blank, imported, solo-template, accepted-invite, and ignored-invite paths, then decide whether to preserve a separate solo-first route.
priorities.rationale: Test the contradictory signal and avoid forcing collaboration onto jobs that first require individual preparation.
priorities.success_signal: A coded evidence memo identifies which intents require solo-first versus collaboration-first paths and changes or confirms the rollout rule.
guardrails: Do not expose workspace content to interviewers
Do not recruit a customer who opted out of research contact

experiments.hypothesis: For new self-serve workspaces with 5-20 intended seats, replacing generic setup with a real-workflow start and a contextual teammate task will increase collaborative first value, which will lift retained activation.
experiments.intervention: Randomize eligible workspaces 50/50. Treatment selects a job, creates a role-relevant workspace, asks the creator to assign a real first task to one named teammate, and sends an invitation containing that task context; control receives current generic setup. Enroll at least 1,900 eligible workspaces; assuming no more than 10% exclusion or attrition leaves about 1,710 analyzed, or 855 per arm. This is approximately 80% powered to detect a mature retained-activation change from 14% to 20% with two-sided alpha 0.05 under a normal approximation. These are planning estimates, not guarantees; segmentation is exploratory unless separately powered. The observed eligible share is 64%; if 1,900 have not enrolled by 2026-08-31, continue enrollment until the target is reached and shift the final decision accordingly. Dependencies: priority 1 scorecard and QA, one product designer, two engineers, lifecycle email access, and stable experiment assignment.
experiments.primary_metric: Percentage of eligible signups reaching retained activation after the complete 28-day observation window
experiments.decision_rule: On 2026-08-20, use collaborative first value, week-2 value, safety, and data-quality checks only to decide whether to continue or stop; do not make a ship decision. On 2026-10-02 or at least 32 days after a later enrollment close, ship to the tested segment only if mature week-4 retained activation in treatment is at least 20%, the absolute treatment-control lift is at least 6 percentage points, the 95% confidence interval for the treatment-control difference excludes zero, and no guardrail is breached. Iterate rather than ship if collaborative first value or week-2 value improves but the mature retained-activation rule is not met. Stop and revisit the diagnosis for a negative mature effect or a guardrail breach.
guardrails: Setup completion decline no worse than 5 percentage points
Invite complaint rate below 0.5%
Severe permission-error rate below 1%
Median support minutes per activated workspace no more than 10% above control
Do not auto-message teammates without creator confirmation
experiments.duration: Planned enrollment 2026-07-27 to 2026-08-31; interim safety decision 2026-08-20; final ship decision no earlier than 2026-10-02 and later if adaptive enrollment continues

handbook_references.title: Activation Handbook
handbook_references.relevance: Defines activation as received value, supports segmented funnel analysis, and requires downstream retention validation.

handbook_references.title: Retention Handbook
handbook_references.relevance: Grounds the week-2/week-4 retained-value guardrail and cohort interpretation.

case_references.title: Activation case comparison
case_references.relevance: Compares value proxies and downstream validation; it does not provide a universal activation threshold.

case_references.title: Airtable
case_references.relevance: Supports intent-specific starts, useful workflows, and collaboration as a quality signal; the simulated product is not Airtable and its metrics are unrelated.

case_references.title: Sean Ellis
case_references.relevance: Supports discovering a product-specific value event and validating it against retention; observational association still requires an experiment.

dependencies: Existing product analytics and experimentation tools

Self-serve method: define and QA the scorecard, join identity/workflow/billing/support data, randomize eligible workspaces, monitor guardrails, conduct consented interviews, freeze the analysis population, and apply the interim and final decision rules
guardrails: Do not collect customer content
Require creator confirmation before teammate messaging
Setup completion decline no worse than 5 percentage points
Invite complaint rate below 0.5%
Severe permission-error rate below 1%
Median support minutes per activated workspace no more than 10% above control
